name: Sherrod Brown
subject: Looking down the road Peter (survey)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
|
---

| | It may not seem like it, but November 2020 will be here before we know it. I've been talking to folks from Ohio and across the country about what they expect from the 2020 race. What issues they think are important. What they plan to do to make sure Trump doesn't win a second term. **And today, I want to hear from you.** [**Peter, take our survey >>**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffc4d/4462dc26/8d38033f/201edf98/3485723248/VEsH/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e190303a") Maybe you've noticed that your paycheck doesn't go as far as it should, and you want your next president to focus on raising wages and creating better-paying jobs. Maybe you've noticed the price of your prescriptions going up, and want your next president to bring the costs down, so more families like yours can get the care they need without facing financial hardship. Maybe you're worried about affording a home, paying for your kids' college educations, or saving for retirement. Whatever it is, [**I want to know what motivates you to head to the polls -- and what you plan to do to help Democrats win in 2020.**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffc4d/4462dc26/8d38033f/201edf98/3485723248/VEsE/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e190303a") | | | [Take our survey](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffc4d/4462dc26/8d38033f/201edf98/3485723248/VEsF/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e190303a")
---
I'm looking forward to reading through your responses.
With gratitude,
Sherrod

|
---
[www.sherrodbrown.com](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffc4d/4462dc26/8d38033f/201edf9f/3485723248/VEsC/ "https://www.sherrodbrown.com/")
| | Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
---

[Click here to unsubscribe](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffc4d/4462dc26/8d38033f/201edf9e/3485723248/VEsD/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/unsubscribe")

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: Why today is so special
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
| | Dear Peter,

Today, March 4th, is a special day in the Spanberger household. In addition to being the most inspiring sounding date on the calendar ("March forth!"), it's also my daughter, Catherine's, birthday.

Many of you might remember her from [**Election Night**](https://act.myngp.com/el/Nv4qhGE6Xm-1j9T7gvAnRhOsKgP4Exm7h2QG-VW7D88=/dzIBUBNJqEspR4_T88Rw59hxuW7vQc1x8M_NL-ZGLgs=). Shortly after I walked on stage to deliver my victory speech, Catherine, startled by the din of the cheering crowd, ran over and sat at my feet as I stood at the podium. Having a nervous child clutching your legs doesn't exactly make public speaking any easier, but maternal instinct told me my baby was scared. So, I did what any mom would do: I picked her up, and we marched forth. But no matter what, she'll always be the child in my arms, giving me strength and reminding me of my purpose.

I didn't run for office because anyone asked me to. I saw problems in our communities that threaten the health, security, and opportunity of the children in our community and millions more across the country. As a mother, I felt compelled to step forth and fight for our next generation to ensure that every child's birthday is a day to celebrate their promise. By ensuring that our nation's children have a quality education and healthcare and feel safe from hunger and violence, we create the opportunity for them to show that promise. As a Congresswoman, that's what drives me.

So, to Catherine and to anyone else celebrating on March 4th, I wish you Happy Birthday, and many, many more.

March forth!

_-Abigail_
| [CONTRIBUTE »](https://act.myngp.com/el/Nv4qhGE6Xm-1j9T7gvAnRhOsKgP4Exm7h2QG-VW7D88=/fs4_OwVdPSMPTXZtPmvUg-8ex76APJnCh_o3YDz9GZs=?refcode=em_fr_2019.03.04_b1_as_all&amount=25)
---

_Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat, former CIA officer, and representative for Virginia 's 7th Congressional District. She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together. To learn more, please visit [AbigailSpanberger.com](https://act.myngp.com/el/Nv4qhGE6Xm-1j9T7gvAnRhOsKgP4Exm7h2QG-VW7D88=/AGhYaIuJprtIGY5cVC5Gx3-XQaMCfZHOq1Ho4IZSFnY=)_

Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
---
Spanberger for Congress
PO Box 3121
Glen Allen VA 23058 United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please [unsubscribe](https://act.myngp.com/el/Nv4qhGE6Xm-1j9T7gvAnRhOsKgP4Exm7h2QG-VW7D88=/7W68IcjQv6veNXSBeIznPn7Molf3CwW-EmG9SDTp614=).

subject: Today is Tina's birthday!

body:

name: Michael Bennett
subject: Bernie understands the real issues that Americans talk about around the dinner table
body:
I supported Senator Bernie Sanders for president in 2016 because we needed an unflinching vision for transforming our world that includes people of all identities and backgrounds.
# [](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a5bd/bed43eb/9b98bfb2/75fb1cd3/219602954/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ourrevolution?refcode=email-header-OR")
Friends,
I supported Senator Bernie Sanders for president in 2016 because we needed an unflinching vision for transforming our world that includes people of all identities and backgrounds.
**In 2020 I believe the same.**
It has been 400 years since my ancestors were shackled in boats and brought here across the Atlantic Ocean; 51 years since Martin Luther King was assassinated; 11 years since Barack Obama was elected; and for the past 3 years we have experienced the disastrous Trump effect. The time is now, not just for change, but for transformation. No more waiting. It's time to act for the future of our children.
Bernie understands the real issues that Americans face and talk about around the dinner table: from bail reform to the mass incarceration of Brown and Black people; from stopping the attacks on immigrants to the need for a living wage; from the lack of funding in marginalized schools, to the increased gun violence in our communities; from ensuring health care for everyone, to wiping out student debt and making college affordable for all. Bernie has bold ideas about how to create a future where young people have the opportunity to thrive.
But Bernie Sanders alone can't bring resources into every underserved community. We need to connect with each other in solidarity, stand together, and continue to fight for what's right. Black, brown, and white, regardless of our gender we ALL have a voice -- LET IT ROAR!
Let it be known that we will stand together and continue to fight for what's right.
You can't build a movement by yourself -- it takes legions of people like you who want to build it. We all need sisters and brothers willing to carry the bricks and the mortar. And we need everyone to offer up their special skills.

**Our Revolution is helping to do the down and dirty movement work to connect people and energize them around the issues that matter most.**
[**By chipping in just $5, $10, or whatever you can afford, you can support this grassroots organizing effort so they can connect people for decades to come.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a5bd/bed43eb/9b98bfb2/75fb1cd2/219602954/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-2020?refcode=em190304-full&noskip=true")
It's through the suffering of our ancestors that we learn our biggest lesson of why we must be committed--Justice.
Like 1968 Olympian John Carlos always says, "There is no partial commitment to justice. You are either in or you're out."
[**Well, I 'm in. Can I count you in too?**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a5bd/bed43eb/9b98bfb2/75fb1cd2/219602954/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-2020?refcode=em190304-full&noskip=true")
In solidarity,
Michael Bennett
Professional Football Player, New York Time Best Selling Author, and Humanitarian
****[DONATE»](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a5bd/bed43eb/9b98bfb2/75fb1cd2/219602954/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-2020?refcode=em190304-full&noskip=true")****
PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
PO Box 66208
WASHINGTON, DC, 20035

Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee. Our Revolution is a 501(c)(4) organization. Donations to Our Revolution are not deductible as charitable contributions for Federal income tax purposes. All donations are made to support Our Revolution's general mission and are not designated for any specific activity.
PO Box 66208, Washington, DC 20035
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you need to update or change your information or email address, click here to [update your info](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a5bd/bed43eb/9b98bfb2/75fb1cdd/219602954/VEsD/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/information-update?source=email_footer"). Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a5bd/bed43eb/9b98bfb2/75fb1cdc/219602954/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjIyMTIzIn0=/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/OR-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=22123"). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a5bd/bed43eb/9b98bfb2/75fb1cdf/219602954/VEsB/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/unsubscribe/"). Help us continue transforming American politics and fighting for progressive candidates and causes by [contributing to Our Revolution here](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a5bd/bed43eb/9b98bfb2/75fb1cde/219602954/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ourrevolution?refcode=email-footer-OR").

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: ‘You Have to Pay With Your Body’: The Hidden Nightmare of Sexual Violence on the Border
body:
"On America’s southern border, migrant women and girls are the victims of sexual assaults that most often go unreported." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=24e396e433&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **‘You Have to Pay With Your Body’: The Hidden Nightmare of Sexual Violence on the Border**

“On America’s southern border, migrant women and girls are the victims of sexual assaults that most often go unreported, uninvestigated and unprosecuted,” Manny Fernandez reports in _The New York Times_.

“The stories are many, and yet all too similar. Undocumented women making their way into American border towns have been beaten for disobeying smugglers, impregnated by strangers, coerced into prostitution, shackled to beds and trees and — in at least a handful of cases — bound with duct tape, rope or handcuffs.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2a7dd707c8&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “America’s factories are hiring again,” Chip Cutter writes in _[The Wall Street Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=197cf90657&e=3ee4985eea)_. “After years of job losses, U.S. manufacturing employment has risen for 18 straight months among those holding production or nonsupervisory jobs, the longest stretch of gains since the mid-1990s. Employers have added 274,000 non-managerial manufacturing jobs since July 2017, Labor Department figures show.”
---
|
---
| [Vice President Mike Pence](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=131f8442e9&e=3ee4985eea) writes that last Friday, “the defense secretary released a legislative proposal at the president’s direction to establish the Space Force, within the Air Force, as the sixth branch of the armed forces . . . Today, the threats and opportunities in space are changing more rapidly than at any other point since the Cold War. And we must change along with them.”
---
|
---
| In Fox News, [White House Director of the Office of Trade and Manufacturing Policy Peter Navarro](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=29444c92cb&e=3ee4985eea) writes that today “President Donald J. Trump will sign an executive order to help sea veterans seamlessly transition into the United States Merchant Marine. By simultaneously expanding veteran opportunities for great jobs at great wages while strengthening our Merchant Marine, this action embodies a key principle of the Trump administration: economic security is national security.”
---
|
---
| “The North Korean political and military Rubik’s Cube has confounded Democratic and Republican administrations for 70 years,” Army Major General Bob Dees writes in _[USA Today](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=abfcca6a5d&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Trump’s courageous decision to walk away from negotiations rather than accept Kim’s demand for sanctions reliefs showed that he will not make the same mistake as his predecessors by offering unreciprocated concessions to the rogue state.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=43ca8c8d4d&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8718d6aec9&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=8e6315c86e)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
---

name: Donald J. Trump
subject: Last day to WIN BIG
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trump Make America Great Again Committee, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Donald J
body:
You haven’t entered yet... |
---
| | | | | | | | [](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=eafc6d997c4ef81e6b31da942ed607bfefdf6d4a9270bdb306179b00b6e8390e3c08053f4ec691e9ad92e618877780803935c2a70c26d8a0)
---

Friend,

Today is your LAST CHANCE to have breakfast with me in beautiful Florida.

My team handed me a list of all patriots who entered to have breakfast with me and I noticed your name was _MISSING._

With my extremely busy schedule, I normally don't even have time for breakfast. But, when my team told me that one lucky American Patriot would be there, I knew I had to make the time.

My entire campaign and Presidency have never been about me** _....it has always been about YOU._**

**Remember if you win, my team will cover your flight, your hotel stay, the meal, and we 'll all take a picture together!**

As one of our top supporters, I’ve decided to unlock a short-term DOUBLE ENTRY for you.

---
| **[Any contribution made by 11:59 PM TONIGHT will enter your name TWICE!](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=eafc6d997c4ef81e8b9b46d759016e3feef4cda82804b949bb51352af11410599de2382059d6064c917da98198f658777623286232de5188)**

name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: End the prohibition on marijuana
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETO FOR TEXAS
body:
It’s unacceptable that our country has the world’s largest prison
population, disproportionately comprised of people of color. One-third of
that prison population is there for nonviolent drug crimes, and though we
know that people of all races use illegal drugs at roughly the same rate,
some are being locked away for it more than others.

Many have called this part of the New Jim Crow, and for good reason.

One in four black children have had a parent in the criminal justice
system, compared to just four percent of white children. That rate is
nearly two times what it was in the 1980s. And it begins with a
school-to-prison pipeline that starts as early as kindergarten, where a
black child is four to five times as likely to be suspended or expelled as
a white child.

I am more convinced than ever that we can and must build a criminal
justice system that is more fair and that urgently puts our country closer
to the words written above the highest court in our land: equal justice
under law.

This is how I propose we do it.

First, we need to end the failed war on drugs that has long been a war on
people, waged on some people over other people. Who is going to be the
last man — more likely than not a black man — to languish behind bars for
possessing or using marijuana when it is legal in some form in more than
half of the states in this country? We should end the federal prohibition
on marijuana and expunge the records of those who were locked away for
possessing it, ensuring that they can get work, finish their education,
contribute to the greatness of this country.

Second, we end the broken system of cash bail that punishes people for
being poor. This is a tactic that wastes resources on incarcerating those
who are not a threat to anyone, not a flight risk, not likely to be repeat
offenders. In the Harris County Jail alone, it’s estimated that 500 to 600
of the inmates at any given time fit this description. And that's not an
outlier -- 75% of people in Texas jails have not been convicted of any
crime but many can't afford bail.

Third, we should eliminate private, for-profit prisons from our justice
system to ensure we're always putting people before profits. Locking
someone up is a power that should be reserved for our government -- the
people, not outsourced to corporations that have the perverse incentive of
getting more men and women behind bars so that there are more profits for
their shareholders.

Fourth, we must stop using mandatory minimum sentencing for non-violent
drug offenses — a practice that costs taxpayers dearly and destroys lives
in the process by locking up people who could safely re-enter society. And
we replace this practice with policies that begin treating addiction like
the public health concern it is.

Finally, we should provide meaningful reentry to help people who have been
incarcerated resume their lives and contribute to their full potential.
That starts with strong rehabilitation services, counseling and access to
preventative health care. It continues by banning the box on job
applications so those formerly incarcerated can work and pay taxes,
returning drivers licenses so they can get to that place of employment,
allowing them to apply for loans that can unlock skills trainings, and
ensuring their constitutional right to participate in civic life by voting
is protected.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/justice-reform/?t=1001&akid=24962%2E444211%2EIyGw_d ]Can you sign your name today to say you agree that we need to make
these meaningful reforms to our broken justice system?

As a congressman, I worked with colleagues across the aisle to introduce
legislation that aims to do these things, to achieve real reforms. I know
we can get this done but only if we all work towards these goals together.

At the end of the day, this is about ensuring that every single one of us
can live to our full potential and contribute to our maximum capacity.
Giving low-level offenders a second chance no matter the color of their
skin or the economic status they hold can create opportunity for all of
us.

It will help build a future that is more just, more fair, and more
prosperous for every single person in this state and this country.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/justice-reform/?t=1002&akid=24962%2E444211%2EIyGw_d ]Add your name to say you support the points below:

We imprison more of our own people than any other country on the planet,
disproportionately African Americans and Latinos. Let’s build a criminal
justice system Americans can trust and that puts our country closer to the
words written above the highest court in our land: equal justice under
law.

1. End the federal prohibition on marijuana and expunge the records of
those who were locked away for possessing it.
2. End the current system of cash bail that punishes people for being
poor.
3. Eliminate private, for-profit prisons from our justice system.
4. End the use of mandatory minimum sentencing for non-violent drug
offenses.
5. Provide meaningful reentry to help people who have been incarcerated
resume their lives and contribute to their full potential.

After you’re done signing your name, make sure to share this email with
friends and family so we can get more people on board with this forward
thinking, equitable vision for reforming our country’s justice system.

- Beto

Contribute to Beto's campaign: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beto-email?refcode=em20190304footer&t=1003&refcode2=24962_444211_IyGw_d&akid=24962%2E444211%2EIyGw_d

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betofortexas.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1004&akid=24962%2E444211%2EIyGw_d

PAID FOR BY BETO FOR TEXAS
P.O. Box 3628 El Paso, TX 79923

name: BernieSanders.com
subject: [Photos] Our YUUUGE events in Brooklyn and Chicago
body:
You’ll want to see this.
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=659%2E150750%2EDHeRju)

Sisters and Brothers -
We're going to win this election not because we have a super PAC funded by billionaires. We're going to win this election because we will put together the strongest grassroots coalition in the history of American politics.
That is the message we sent in Brooklyn at our first official campaign event.
And that is the message we sent last night in Chicago at the second rally of our campaign.
While Donald Trump wants to divide us up by the color of our skin, our country of origin, our gender, our religion and our sexual orientation – we are going to do exactly the opposite. We are going to bring our people together – black, white, Latino, Native American, Asian American, gay and straight, young and old, men and women, native born and immigrant.
Because when we do that, we are going to finally create an economy and a government which works for all Americans, and not just the 1 percent.
In solidarity,
Team Bernie
* * *

**The wealthy and the powerful will do everything possible to defend their financial interests. They have unlimited money, but we have each other. Not just Bernie – us.

Add your name to say you are committed to do whatever it takes to win this election, beat Donald Trump, and elect a government that works for all of us, not just the wealthy few.**

[ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/em_rally_signup/?source=em190304-1-120&t=2&akid=659%2E150750%2EDHeRju)

Paid for by Bernie 2020
PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you need to, [unsubscribe using this link](https://bernie.actionkit.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=3&akid=659%2E150750%2EDHeRju). But we hope you'll stay, because emails are such an important part of how we organize and how we raise money. In fact, there is no single greater source for our campaign's fundraising than emails like this one. And that's important, because no one person, not even Bernie Sanders, can take on Trump and the billionaire class alone.

Before you go about the rest of your day, help power our fight against the billionaire class by [making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-email-template?refcode=em-footer&t=4&refcode2=659_150750_DHeRju&akid=659%2E150750%2EDHeRju).

name: Kamala Harris
subject: Sign my petition demanding action on gun violence
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KAMALA HARRIS FOR THE PEOPLE
body:
|
#
I have really big news. Last week, the House of Representatives passed the first major piece of legislation on gun violence in a generation. It’s called HR 8. Now, it heads to the Senate. This bill would mandate background checks for every gun purchase in this country. It goes a long way toward making sure people with criminal records or a history of mental illness can’t get their hands on a gun. **Right now, I need your help in making sure this bill gets a vote on the Senate floor. We know the NRA and the gun lobby will do everything in their power to stop it. That’s why we need to come together to show the strength of our movement.** **[Add your name next to mine to demand a vote this week in the Senate on legislation to require background checks on all firearm sales in the country.](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/end-gun-violence?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190304-full&source=ema190304-full&t=1&akid=3287%2E5755789%2E7NHis7)** | | [Sign the petition](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/end-gun-violence?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190304-full&source=ema190304-full&t=2&akid=3287%2E5755789%2E7NHis7)
---
We can’t lose sight of how important this moment is in our history, and how hard we have worked to get here.
A little more than one year ago, the students from Marjory Stoneman Douglas High School led millions of people in marches across the country to demand action on gun violence.
And throughout 2018, people in communities across our nation registered voters, knocked on doors, and organized to elect leaders who are willing to stand up to the gun lobby and do what’s right.
Last week, new members of Congress like Rep. Lucy McBath -- who became an activist for social justice after her son was shot and killed -- voted to pass the first significant legislation on gun violence in 20 years.
**This is a great step forward. When we work together, arm in arm, to organize and raise our voices to demand action, we can win. But our fight is not yet done. I need you to add your voice right now:**
**[Sign now if you agree: Congress should act swiftly to pass universal background checks»](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/end-gun-violence?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190304-full&source=ema190304-full&t=3&akid=3287%2E5755789%2E7NHis7)**
| [Sign the petition](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/end-gun-violence?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190304-full&source=ema190304-full&t=4&akid=3287%2E5755789%2E7NHis7)
---
This is just the first of many fights to come in our work to end gun violence. I know that by working together, organizing on every block in every community of this country, we can take on the gun lobby and win.
Thanks for standing with me on this. I’ll see you out there.
For The People,
— Kamala Harris

| [Sign the petition](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/end-gun-violence?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190304-full&source=ema190304-full&t=5&akid=3287%2E5755789%2E7NHis7)
---

PAID FOR BY KAMALA HARRIS FOR THE PEOPLE
---
This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com). You received this message because you are subscribed to Senator Kamala Harris' mailing list.
You can [adjust your email preferences here](https://action.kamalaharris.org/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=6&akid=3287%2E5755789%2E7NHis7), or [unsubscribe from receiving further emails here.](https://action.kamalaharris.org/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=3287%2E5755789%2E7NHis7)

Right now, I need your help in making sure this bill gets a vote on the Senate floor. We know the NRA and the gun lobby will do everything in their power to stop it. That’s why we need to come together to show the strength of our movement. | | We can’t lose sight of how important this moment is in our history, and how hard we have worked to get here. A little more than one year ago, the students from Marjory Stoneman Douglas High School led millions of people in marches across the country to demand action on gun violence. And throughout 2018, people in communities across our nation registered voters, knocked on doors, and organized to elect leaders who are willing to stand up to the gun lobby and do what’s right. Last week, new members of Congress like Rep. Lucy McBath -- who became an activist for social justice after her son was shot and killed -- voted to pass the first significant legislation on gun violence in 20 years. This is a great step forward. When we work together, arm in arm, to organize and raise our voices to demand action, we can win. But our fight is not yet done. I need you to add your voice right now: | This is just the first of many fights to come in our work to end gun violence. I know that by working together, organizing on every block in every community of this country, we can take on the gun lobby and win. Thanks for standing with me on this. I’ll see you out there. For The People, — Kamala Harris | PAID FOR BY KAMALA HARRIS FOR THE PEOPLE

name: Nicole Stanton
subject: please join us!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stanton for Congress
body:
Friend,

These first two months since Greg was sworn in have been a time of transition for our family but, we all could not be more proud of how hard Greg is working for all of us in Washington.

The craziest part of all of this though? The work to ensure Greg gets re-elected starts now and [we need your help.](https://act.myngp.com/el/h_4CxfbTpURj7aCVMjg2TcyrNyHARYBkf_pNRDHeYus=/FGnbKPe5HrPX05vCt_tevmisVkoG7spJSSgO9VaSyck=)

So, what better way to kick off Greg's re-election effort than with a fundraiser celebrating his 49th birthday this Saturday, March 9th from 10 AM - 12 PM at the Yard at Farmers Arts District in Tempe?

**[To donate and RSVP now, click here](https://act.myngp.com/el/h_4CxfbTpURj7aCVMjg2TcyrNyHARYBkf_pNRDHeYus=/FGnbKPe5HrPX05vCt_tevmisVkoG7spJSSgO9VaSyck=). **

**Or **

**[If you want to just RSVP now and make a contribution at the event click here.](https://act.myngp.com/el/h_4CxfbTpURj7aCVMjg2TcyrNyHARYBkf_pNRDHeYus=/ky1qSgvKAvuBfpLIu6idRzb-pWw-u90F7lhjGHLeXqQ=)**

I know it will mean a lot to Greg to be surrounded by family and good friends on this day. If you have any questions please contact Ellie Perez at (602) 434-0974 or via email at Ellie@StantonforArizona.com.

Hope to see you there,
Nicole

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/h_4CxfbTpURj7aCVMjg2TcyrNyHARYBkf_pNRDHeYus=/0_PeC5zR1_TQRouzduPMMoXOPMcYos9xpQ8gBVY1lqI=)

Paid for by Stanton for Congress
